kept.text:
⚾️ CUBS vs. PADRES MLB BEST BET

📈 FERNANDO TATÍS JR. OVER 1.5 TOTAL BASES
👇 Add Directly To Your Betslip
  https://t.co/pQFTratLDG

🗣️ "Fernando Tatis Jr. has had a rough start to the season, having yet to hit a single home run. But it’s only a matter of time until he finds his rhythm at the plate, and it’s a good time to buy low on San Diego’s slugger. Jameson Taillon is set to take the mound for Chicago, and he’s also had a tough start to the season, posting a 4.55 ERA and 1.30 WHIP across his first five outings. Taillon just can’t sustain velocity on his four-seamer anymore, so he’s been allowing more hard contact than ever — he’s already allowed seven homers. He’s also struggling with his command and control. Both are major issues for a guy who relies on precision pitching and ground balls. In six career at-bats against Taillon, Tatis is 2-for-6 with a home run and a double. It is only a matter of time until the Padres’ slugger finds form at the plate, and I think Wednesday afternoon presents a prime opportunity for him to do so versus a pitcher he has already gotten the best of in previous encounters." - @VegasMinion

kept.text:
Let’s say the Westbrook triple double bet was posted on Fanduel (+5000). You think the implied probability of Westbrook getting 8 and 8 is +800 (11.11%), but the Fanduel line is +600 (14.29%). That would be a -EV bet. 

However, let’s say 8 and 8 was (+1500) on DraftKings. That means Westbrook getting 8 and 8 on Fanduel is a -EV bet, but Westbrook getting 8 and 8 on DraftKings is a +EV bet. 

No winning bettor will ever recommend to blindly “water down” a bet without looking at the price.
